ops.title: Testing local capt-hook edits — uvx runs the published wheel (false green); release captain-hook before consumers import new symbols
ops.body:
`uvx capt-hook test` / `uvx capt-hook run` resolve the **published PyPI** captain-hook (cached in `~/.cache/uv`), NOT local working-tree edits — so they report a **false green** that silently ignores local changes. To test local captain-hook edits use `uv run capt-hook test` (inside the captain-hook repo) or `uvx --from /Users/yasyf/Code/captain-hook capt-hook --hooks <pack-dir> test` (for a consumer pack such as cc-context's `plugin/hooks`). `task test` does not exist in captain-hook (no Taskfile) — CI runs `uv run pytest`.

Release ordering: a consumer pack (e.g. cc-context `ccx_guards.py`) that does `from captain_hook import <new symbol>` raises `ImportError` against an older installed captain-hook, and the loader **silently skips the entire pack** — disabling *all* its guards, not just the new one. So release captain-hook FIRST, then deploy consumer packs that use new APIs. See [[cc-context-architecture]].

**A persistent `uv tool install capt-hook` shadows "latest" and does NOT auto-upgrade (2026-07-14).** `uvx capt-hook` = `uv tool run capt-hook`; if capt-hook is installed as a tool (`~/.local/share/uv/tools/capt-hook/`), uvx **reuses that pinned install** rather than resolving PyPI latest — so it can run an arbitrarily OLD version (found v9.2.0 while latest was 9.13.0). This is a distinct trap from the ephemeral-uvx one above: there uvx runs published-not-local; here uvx runs installed-not-latest. It manifested as cc-notes task `ebf5434`: a consumer's `uvx capt-hook test` showed `ImportError: cannot import name 'touched_comment_blocks'` (added in capt-hook 9.10.0) even though the symbol WAS published — the local tool predated 9.10.0. The published wheel and CI (which resolve fresh) were fine; only the stale local install broke. Fix: `uv tool upgrade capt-hook` (check with `uv tool list`). When a consumer's `uvx capt-hook test` shows import errors for a symbol you can confirm is published, suspect a stale `uv tool` install before suspecting an upstream gap.
